records.ety: [OE. desarmen, F. dsarmer; pref. ds- (L. dis-) + armer to arm. See Arm.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disarming (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disarming.]

defs: To deprive of arms; to take away the weapons of; to deprive of the means of attack or defense; to render defenseless.
quotes: The proud was half disarmed of pride.

defs: To deprive of the means or the disposition to harm; to render harmless or innocuous; as, to disarm a man's wrath.
